fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 77 uses strict inequalities (`b <= s` and `a >= e`), which treats touching endpoints (e.g., new end == existing start) as non-overlapping. The correct check should use strict inequalities `b < s or a > e` to treat touching as a conflict.

spec.expr: any(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline) == any(not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) for s, e in timeline) or any(b__1 == s or a__1 == e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: Detect when touching endpoints are incorrectly classified as non-overlapping by the strict-boundary condition.

spec.expr: not any(b__1 == s for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When new window end equals an existing window start, the overlap check must detect a conflict.

spec.expr: not any(a__1 == e for s, e in timeline) or any(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: When new window start equals an existing window end, the overlap check must detect a conflict.

spec.expr: all(b__1 < s or a__1 > e for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: After deciding no conflict exists, verify that the new window truly does not touch or overlap any existing booking.
